signals.title: EUR/USD — Geopolitical Shock + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1765 testing downside as Trump rejects Iran peace offer, escalating West Asia war fears. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% provides a +175bp carry advantage to USD, while EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation (corr=+0.708 vs norm=-0.135, z=+1.87σ) indicates the pair is now hyper-sensitive to European equity stress. COT LF net short -21,011 confirms institutional bias remains against the Euro as energy supply risks intensify. Implication: Geopolitical escalation favors USD safe-haven and carry flows over EUR.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ market bias signaling a hike. With NZD long% at an extreme low of 18.5%, the pair is highly vulnerable to a short-squeeze if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise on 27 May. NZD/USD at 0.5950 remains supported by a +150bp carry premium over USD, yet specs remain crowded on the short side. Implication: Extreme positioning suggests a floor near 0.5900; watch for short-covering on any hawkish RBNZ rhetoric.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7234 faces a positioning bottleneck as COT LF net long reaches +58,994 (78.3% long) while retail sentiment is 89% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence suggests the trend is mature; retail is aggressively fading the move while specs are heavily committed. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% (+60bp AUD advantage) sustains the fundamental bid, but China's export surge (+14.1%) may already be priced in. Implication: Late-trend caution; retail capitulation or a spec unwind poses reversal risk if 0.7200 fails to hold.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7786 shows retail sentiment at 85% long, diverging from the COT LF net short of -7,392. While Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% offers a massive +375bp carry cushion for USD, the extreme retail long positioning acts as a contrarian indicator for further downside. VIX at 17.2 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant, but any spike in West Asia tensions would rapidly bid the CHF safe-haven, punishing the crowded retail long. Implication: Downside pressure persists despite carry advantage; watch for retail liquidation.
